name: Goodreads: Shelf / Genre Top Books
summary: List the most-popular books on a Goodreads shelf/genre (e.g. parenting, self-help, philosophy), paginated. Returns title, author, URL, average rating, ratings count, and publication year.
description: Opens /shelf/show/<shelf> (popularity-ranked) and reads the classic server-rendered listing (a.bookTitle / a.authorName / .greyText). Walks up to max_pages additional pages via same-origin fetch and returns a deduplicated, ranked book list. This is the discovery entry point for 'top books in a field' — feed each book_url into goodreads/book, goodreads/reviews, or goodreads/similar.

shelf.description: Shelf / genre slug as it appears in /shelf/show/<slug>, e.g. 'parenting', 'self-help', 'philosophy', 'psychology', 'business'. Multi-word shelves use hyphens.

max_pages.description: How many pages to collect (50 books per page). Default 1 (top 50). Pass up to 5 for the top 250.

start_page.description: First shelf page to collect (1-based). Default 1. Use this to fetch one clean 50-book page at a time (e.g. start_page=2, max_pages=1 returns books 51-100) and avoid the ~50-row result-array cap when you need more than 50 books across several calls.

inputs.script: const norm=(s)=>(s||'').replace(/\s+/g,' ').trim();const origin=location.origin;const cleanArg=(v)=>{const t=String(v==null?'':v).trim();return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t)?'':t;};const cleanPath=(h)=>{const m=(h||'').match(/\/book\/show\/[^?#]+/);return m?m[0]:'';};const sleep=(ms)=>new Promise(r=>setTimeout(r,ms));const shelfArg=cleanArg(arg0);const maxPages=Math.min(Math.max(parseInt(cleanArg(arg1)||'1',10)||1,1),5);const startPage=Math.min(Math.max(parseInt(cleanArg(arg2)||'1',10)||1,1),25);const parseDoc=(root)=>{const out=[];for(const a of root.querySelectorAll('a.bookTitle')){const cont=a.closest('.elementList')||a.parentElement||root;const path=cleanPath(a.getAttribute('href'));if(!path)continue;const author=norm(cont.querySelector('a.authorName')?.textContent);const grey=norm(cont.querySelector('.greyText.smallText')?.textContent);const rm=grey.match(/avg rating ([\d.]+)/i);const cm=grey.match(/([\d,]+) ratings?/i);const pm=grey.match(/published (\d{4})/i);out.push({title:norm(a.textContent),author:author||'N/A',book_url:origin+path,avg_rating:rm?rm[1]:'N/A',ratings_count:cm?cm[1].replace(/,/g,''):'N/A',published:pm?pm[1]:'N/A'});}return out;};const seen=new Set();const books=[];const addAll=(arr)=>{for(const b of arr){if(seen.has(b.book_url))continue;seen.add(b.book_url);books.push(b);}};let pagesWalked=0;const base=origin+location.pathname.replace(/[?#].*$/,'');const endPage=startPage+maxPages-1;for(let p=startPage;p<=endPage;p++){let doc;if(p===1){doc=document;}else{const r=await fetch(base+'?page='+p,{credentials:'same-origin'});if(!r.ok)break;const html=await r.text();doc=new DOMParser().parseFromString(html,'text/html');}const arr=parseDoc(doc);if(!arr.length)break;addAll(arr);pagesWalked++;await sleep(400);}const shelfName=shelfArg||(location.pathname.match(/\/shelf\/show\/([^/?#]+)/)||[])[1]||'N/A';return {shelf:shelfName,pages_walked:pagesWalked,count:books.length,books:books};

steps.name: Extract ranked book listing; walk extra pages if requested

help.summary: Popularity-ranked book list for a Goodreads shelf/genre, paginated.

parameters.shelf: Shelf/genre slug from /shelf/show/<slug> (e.g. parenting, self-help, philosophy).
parameters.max_pages: Pages to collect, 50 books each (1-5). Default 1.

examples.command: rzn-browser run goodreads shelf --param shelf="self-help" --param max_pages="3"

returns: Object: { shelf, pages_walked, count, books: [{ title, author, book_url, avg_rating, ratings_count, published }] }. Books are ordered by Goodreads' shelf popularity. Feed book_url into goodreads/book, goodreads/reviews, or goodreads/similar.
notes: Shelves are server-rendered classic pages; pagination uses ?page=<n> via same-origin fetch (more reliable than clicking).
Results are deduplicated by book URL across pages.
Shelf popularity ranking is Goodreads' own (how many users shelved the book), a good proxy for 'top books in a field'.
